'''Narro Sienar''' was [[Chief Executive Officer/Legends|CEO]] of [[Santhe/Sienar Technologies]] during [[Great Peace of the Republic|the last years]] of the [[Galactic Republic/Legends|Galactic Republic]], and the father of [[Raith Sienar/Legends|Raith Sienar]] and a [[Narro Sienar's daughter|daughter]].<ref name="Darth Plagueis" />

At some point around between [[97 BBY]] and [[95 BBY]],<ref name="Date">[[Darth Plagueis (novel)|''Darth Plagueis'']] indicates that [[Kerred Santhe]]'s purchase of the company took place thirty [[Standard year|years]] prior to a section of the novel dated to between [[67 BBY/Legends|67 BBY]] and [[65 BBY/Legends|65 BBY]]. Therefore, the purchase must have taken place around between those two years.</ref> Narro was a ship designer for the company when it took control of [[Lianna/Legends|Lianna]] and it came into the hands of [[Liann]] industrialist [[Kerred Santhe]] who purchased a controlling interest and renamed it Santhe/Sienar Technologies. He was forced to arrange for his young daughter to marry Kerred in order to maintain his position as chief designer. Sienar conspired with [[Darth Tenebrous/Legends|Rugess Nome]], from whom [[Santhe Security]], on Kerred's orders, had stolen the starship designs that secured his rise, to have Kerred assassinated, which was done at the hand of [[Darth Plagueis/Legends|Hego Damask]] during a banquet, using [[bloat eel]] as cover. Around [[65 BBY]], Sienar attended a [[Gathering (Sojourn)|Gathering]] on [[Sojourn]], and Damask hoped to position him as CEO of Santhe/Sienar, a position at that point occupied by the assassinated Kerred Santhe's [[Kerred Santhe the Second|son]], who had accrued massive gambling debts.<ref name="Darth Plagueis" />

At some point, despite being the owner of one of the [[Corellian Engineering Corporation/Legends|Corellian Engineering Corporation's]] primary competitors, Sienar helped the Corporation design the successful [[YT-1300 light freighter/Legends|YT-1300 light freighter]].<ref name="Millennium Falcon">[[Millennium Falcon (novel)|''Millennium Falcon'']]</ref> He attended another Gathering sometime between [[54 BBY/Legends|54 BBY]] and [[54 BBY/Legends|52 BBY]], by which point he had become CEO of Santhe/Sienar.<ref name="Darth Plagueis" /> His son Raith abandoned the family in his teenage years to make his own way as a [[Scout/Legends|hyperspace explorer]] using his inheritance. When, after the age of 20, he returned boasting many impressive breakthroughs as well as his own personal fortune, his precocious success impressed his father enough to begin grooming him as his successor. As part of this process, Raith went undercover among many rival companies to collect their secrets, including the [[Xi Char/Legends|Xi Char]] [[Xi Char Cathedral factories/Legends|factory-cathedrals]]. He was however discovered by the secretive and devout insectoids, who, motivated by their religious beliefs which forbad nonbelievers access to trade secrets, sought to kill him. He foiled the attempt and returned to the fold, where he was given his own workshop, [[Sienar Design Systems/Legends|Sienar Design Systems]]. This feud with the Xi Char however would claim Narro's life, as in [[38 BBY/Legends|38 BBY]], while Narro and Raith were were in a pleasure cruise near [[Dantooine/Legends|Dantooine]], a group of mercenaries hired by the [[Xi Charrian/Legends|Xi Charrians]] destroyed their vessel. Narro was killed in the attack, while Raith escaped in an escape ship and was given control of the company by the board of directors.<ref name="NEC">''[[The New Essential Guide to Characters]]''</ref>
